sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The state is the same as before - clicking the blue shape again didn't change anything (except the white strip went from 58 to 56, which is likely a timer/counter). So clicking the blue shape only had an effect the first time.
Let me reconsider the game mechanics. I've been making random clicks and wasting actions. Let me think about this more systematically.
Current state:
- 4 colored squares at top: S, M, p, Y
- Yellow 4×4 square in top bar
- Blue circular shape in center
- 2 pink (M) 2×2 squares (from clicked dots)
- 4 remaining sky blue (S) 1-pixel dots
The target is about 42 actions. I've used 5 so far (including wasted ones). Let me think about what could require 42 actions:
- Maybe I need to click on each of the 4 remaining dots multiple times
- Maybe I need to click on the colored squares to "select" a color, then click on dots
- Maybe there's a pattern to follow
Wait, let me reconsider. In level 1, I clicked on gray dots along a path to move a purple square. The mechanic was "click on a path element to advance." Maybe in level 2, the mechanic is similar but different.
Let me look at the dots' positions relative to each other and the blue shape:
- [54, 49], [55, 14], [56, 47], [57, 16] - these are all at the bottom
- The two M squares are at [36-37, 17-18] and [36-37, 40-41]
- The blue shape is at [23-31, 29-37]
Maybe the dots form a pattern that needs to be "completed" by clicking them in a specific order. Or maybe I need to click on the colored squares first to select a color, then click on dots to paint them.
Let me try a different approach: click on one of the remaining sky blue dots and see what happens now (since I've already clicked the blue shape).
